pairs.question: When was Booker T. Washington Junior College established and by whom?
pairs.answer: Booker T. Washington Junior College was established in 1949 by the Escambia County school board.

pairs.question: What were the higher education options available to African Americans in Florida before 1949?
pairs.answer: Before 1949, the higher education options available to African Americans in Florida were Bethune-Cookman College, Edward Waters College, Florida A&M University, and Florida Memorial College, all of which were historically black institutions.

pairs.question: Who was the founding president, dean, and principal of Booker T. Washington Junior College?
pairs.answer: The founding president, dean, and principal of Booker T. Washington Junior College was Garrett T. Wiggins. He was the only educator in northwest Florida with an earned doctorate and was described as 'the smartest man in Escambia County'.

pairs.question: What is the significance of Booker T. Washington Junior College in relation to Booker T. Washington High School?
pairs.answer: Booker T. Washington Junior College shared facilities and administrators with Booker T. Washington High School in Pensacola, Florida. The college was named in honor of the famous black intellectual Booker T. Washington.

pairs.question: How many students graduated in the first class of Booker T. Washington Junior College and in what year?
pairs.answer: The first class of Booker T. Washington Junior College, consisting of 23 students, graduated in 1951.

pairs.question: What was the peak enrollment of Booker T. Washington Junior College?
pairs.answer: At its peak, Booker T. Washington Junior College enrolled 361 students.

pairs.question: Why was Booker T. Washington Junior College closed in 1965?
pairs.answer: Booker T. Washington Junior College was closed in 1965 in response to the pressures for integration during the Civil Rights Movement.
